East Africa

Kenya Travel Guide

Best for first safari travelers, migration season planning, and itineraries that combine wildlife with a short coast extension.

General information about Kenya

Kenya remains the clearest first-time safari gateway thanks to strong guide networks, the Maasai Mara, private conservancies, and smooth access from Nairobi.

Getting around

Nairobi is the main hub for domestic bush flights, private transfers, and overland loops into the Mara, Amboseli, Laikipia, and the coast.

Best time to visit

July to October is peak migration season, while January to March is excellent for clearer weather and strong game viewing.

Planning notes

Use this country page to compare attractions, understand how to move between them, and decide whether the trip works best as a standalone route or part of a broader regional circuit.

Country at a glance

Kenya

  • 2 featured attractions seeded
  • 1 active tour operator profiles
  • 2 accommodations nearby
  • 2 recommended restaurants

Attractions

Tourist attractions in Kenya

Each listing opens into a full independent attraction page with gallery, practical information, how to get there, nearby stays, restaurants, and booking links.

Image slot Attraction Maasai Mara

Kenya

Maasai Mara

Kenya’s classic safari icon for migration drama, predators, and broad savannah horizons.

★ 4.9 (1,384) From $95
Southwestern Kenya
View Details
Image slot Attraction Amboseli National Park

Kenya

Amboseli National Park

Big-elephant country with unforgettable Kilimanjaro backdrops.

★ 4.8 (724) From $70
Southern Kenya
View Details

Tour operators

Tour operators active in Kenya

Kenya Journey Studio

A locally grounded tour operator active in Kenya, helping travelers combine attractions, stays, transport, and realistic route timing rather than selling disconnected components.

Country tours Transfers Private guiding Attraction booking support
Visit operator

Nearby stays

Accommodations near the main attractions

Image slot Stay Governors Camp

Maasai Mara

Governors' Camp

A historic tented camp in a strong game-viewing zone, often used by travelers who want classic Mara atmosphere without losing comfort.

★ 4.7 (684) From $620 / person
Safari tented camp
View Details
Image slot Stay Ol Tukai Lodge Amboseli

Amboseli National Park

Ol Tukai Lodge Amboseli

A well-known Amboseli base for elephant viewing and Kilimanjaro-facing safari days.

★ 4.6 (477) From $300 / night
Safari lodge
View Details

Dining

Recommended restaurants near each attraction

Image slot Restaurant Governors Camp Dining

Maasai Mara

Governors' Camp Dining

The Mara is not a city restaurant destination; dining is usually tied to camp quality, guide timing, and whether bush meals are included.

★ 4.6 (311) $$$
Safari camp dining
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Ol Tukai Lodge Dining

Amboseli National Park

Ol Tukai Lodge Dining

Ol Tukai dining works because visitors are usually inside the park for early and late game drives, making lodge meals more realistic than off-site restaurant runs.

★ 4.4 (205) $$
Safari lodge dining
View Details
